{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "A global logistics platform expanding driver-facing compensation visibility across three jurisdictions with fundamentally different labor classifications, legal mandates, and institutional approval processes. This is not a single feature — it is three jurisdiction-specific implementations unified by a shared UI concept.",
    "bounded_context": "Driver Compensation / Earnings Transparency",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Base Pay", "definition": "The guaranteed compensation a driver receives for accepting and completing a delivery, independent of tips or bonuses. For employees (CA), this must meet or exceed minimum wage floors. For contractors (ID), this is the agreed platform rate." },
      { "term": "Per-Delivery Bonus", "definition": "Additional compensation awarded per completed delivery, often conditional on surge pricing, distance thresholds, or promotional campaigns." },
      { "term": "Tip", "definition": "A voluntary gratuity from the customer to the driver. In California, tips must NOT be counted toward minimum wage obligations (Labor Code §351). Must be displayed as a line item separate from platform-determined pay." },
      { "term": "Deduction", "definition": "Any amount subtracted from gross earnings. CRITICALLY: the nature of deductions differs by driver classification. For employees (CA): payroll taxes, benefits contributions, garnishments. For contractors (ID): platform commission, equipment fees, cash-on-delivery reconciliation. These are not the same concept and must not be conflated." },
      { "term": "Earnings Breakdown", "definition": "The itemized real-time view showing all components that sum to net earnings for a given period." },
      { "term": "Works Council (Betriebsrat)", "definition": "A legally mandated employee representative body in Germany with co-determination rights. Under BetrVG §87(1)(6), introduction or modification of technical systems that monitor employee performance or behavior requires works council consent BEFORE deployment." },
      { "term": "Driver Classification", "definition": "The legal status of the driver — employee or independent contractor — which determines applicable labor law, tax treatment, and what the platform is legally required or permitted to display." },
      { "term": "Net Earnings", "definition": "The final amount a driver receives after all deductions are applied to gross earnings. Must always equal the arithmetic sum of all positive and negative line items." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "The sum of all displayed earnings components (base pay + bonuses + tips - deductions) must exactly equal the displayed net earnings total. No rounding discrepancy, no hidden adjustments.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Arithmetic integrity of financial data. If the breakdown does not sum to the total, the system is lying to the driver. This is a data consistency invariant regardless of jurisdiction.",
      "testable_assertion": "For any driver, for any time period, SUM(base_pay + per_delivery_bonus + tips - deductions) == displayed_net_earnings, with zero tolerance."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "In California, the earnings breakdown MUST be displayed to all drivers. This is not an opt-in feature — it is a legal obligation under wage transparency requirements. Drivers classified as employees under AB5 must see itemized base pay, overtime (if applicable), bonuses, tips (separate from wages), and all deductions with descriptions.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Legal compliance. Failure to display constitutes a labor law violation exposing the platform to penalties, lawsuits, and PAGA claims. The system cannot legally operate in CA without this.",
      "testable_assertion": "Every active California driver sees a complete earnings breakdown including all legally required line items within any earnings view. No CA driver can be served a view that omits any component."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "In California, tips MUST be displayed as a line item fully separate from base pay and MUST NOT be factored into minimum wage calculations.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "California Labor Code §351 prohibits employers from crediting tips against wage obligations. Displaying them in a way that suggests they contribute to base compensation is legally non-compliant.",
      "testable_assertion": "In the CA earnings breakdown, tips appear as an independent line item. The base pay line item alone (excluding tips) meets or exceeds the applicable minimum wage rate multiplied by hours worked."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "In Germany, the earnings transparency feature MUST NOT be deployed to any driver until the applicable Betriebsrat has provided documented approval. The system must support a jurisdiction-level feature gate that blocks rollout independently of code readiness.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "BetrVG §87(1)(6) grants works councils co-determination rights over technical monitoring systems. Deploying without consent is illegal and can result in injunctions, the feature being forcibly disabled, and damaged labor relations.",
      "testable_assertion": "No German driver can access the earnings breakdown feature while the Germany jurisdiction feature gate is set to 'pending_works_council_approval'. The gate can only be changed by an authorized compliance action, not by a deploy."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "Each deduction line item must include a human-readable description explaining what the deduction is for. Opaque deductions (e.g., 'Other: -$4.50') are prohibited.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "An unexplained deduction in a transparency feature defeats the purpose of the feature and, in employee jurisdictions, violates itemized wage statement requirements.",
      "testable_assertion": "Every deduction line item has a non-empty description field that matches a known deduction category. No deduction may be displayed with a generic or empty label."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "Earnings data must be displayed in the local currency of the jurisdiction where the driver operates (USD for California, EUR for Germany, IDR for Indonesia).",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Displaying earnings in a foreign currency misrepresents actual compensation and creates confusion. Compensation is always denominated in the currency of the labor relationship.",
      "testable_assertion": "A driver operating in Indonesia sees all earnings amounts in IDR. A driver in CA sees USD. A driver in Germany sees EUR. No currency conversion is applied at the display layer."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "In Indonesia, displaying the earnings breakdown is a business decision (not legally mandated). The platform may choose the level of detail shown. However, if shown, BR-1 (arithmetic integrity) and BR-5 (deduction labeling) still apply.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Indonesian law does not mandate pay transparency for independent contractors. The scope and detail of the breakdown is a product/policy decision that leadership could change.",
      "testable_assertion": "If the earnings breakdown is displayed to an Indonesian driver, the sum of components equals net earnings and all deductions are labeled. The feature may be toggled on/off for Indonesia without legal consequence."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "The deduction taxonomy must be jurisdiction-specific. Employee deductions (payroll tax, benefits, garnishments) and contractor deductions (platform commission, equipment rental, COD reconciliation) are distinct domain concepts and must not share a single undifferentiated model.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Conflating employee and contractor deductions in a single model risks misclassifying the labor relationship. If an Indonesian contractor sees 'payroll tax withheld,' it implies employment. If a CA employee sees 'platform commission,' it implies contracting. Either creates legal exposure for misclassification.",
      "testable_assertion": "The deduction categories available for CA drivers are drawn exclusively from an employee-deduction taxonomy. The deduction categories for ID drivers are drawn exclusively from a contractor-deduction taxonomy. There is no shared 'generic deduction' type used across both."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [
    {
      "id": "AC-1",
      "given": "A driver in California completes a delivery",
      "when": "They view their earnings for the current period",
      "then": "They see an itemized breakdown showing base pay, per-delivery bonus, tips (as a separate line), and all deductions (each labeled), summing exactly to net earnings — in USD"

      "when": "They view their earnings",

    {

    },
    {
      "id": "AC-6",

      "when": "Compared against minimum wage × hours worked",
      "then": "Base pay alone meets or exceeds the minimum wage floor. Tips are never included in this calculation."
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-7",

      "when": "A new delivery is completed or a tip is received",
      "then": "The earnings breakdown updates to reflect the new data within the platform's defined latency threshold (see escalation — 'real time' is undefined)"
    }
  ],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "A California driver has deductions exceeding gross earnings for a period (e.g., benefit repayment clawback), resulting in negative net earnings", "expected_behavior": "The breakdown must still display honestly. Negative net earnings are shown with a clear explanation. The system must NOT suppress or hide negative periods — wage statement laws require accuracy, not positivity." },
    { "scenario": "A tip is adjusted or reversed after initial display (customer disputes, fraud detection)", "expected_behavior": "The earnings breakdown must update to reflect the adjustment, with the adjustment appearing as a distinct line item (e.g., 'Tip adjustment') rather than silently modifying the original tip amount. Audit trail must be preserved." },
    { "scenario": "A driver relocates from Indonesia to Germany mid-employment", "expected_behavior": "Historical earnings in Indonesia display with IDR and contractor deduction taxonomy. Earnings from Germany onward display in EUR with employee deduction taxonomy. The transition point must be clean — no hybrid period where both taxonomies apply simultaneously." },
    { "scenario": "Germany works council approves the feature but with modifications (e.g., 'show base pay and bonuses but not per-delivery metrics')", "expected_behavior": "The system must support partial or conditional display configurations per jurisdiction.     { "scenario": "Platform applies a new deduction type that has not been mapped to the jurisdiction-specific taxonomy", "expected_behavior": "The system must reject or quarantine the unmapped deduction rather than displaying it with a generic label.     { "scenario": "Multiple deliveries complete near-simultaneously and earnings update in real time", "expected_behavior": "The displayed breakdown must remain arithmetically consistent (BR-1) at every render. Partial updates that temporarily show incorrect totals violate the invariant. Either batch the updates atomically or use optimistic rendering that maintains sum integrity." },
    { "scenario": "Indonesian driver receives a cash-on-delivery payment and platform deducts commission from it", "expected_behavior": "The COD flow must be represented in the breakdown: gross delivery amount, platform commission deduction, net to driver — all in IDR with clear labels. The driver must understand why the deposited amount differs from the delivery price." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "California non-compliance with wage transparency and AB5 carries exposure to PAGA representative action lawsuits (potentially millions in penalties across the driver pool), plus per-paycheck penalties under Labor Code §226 for deficient wage statements.
